title: Code for "Rapid and Quantitative Phage Susceptibility Test by Ramanome"
author: Han, Xiao
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
# Source Code for the Composite Infection Index: A Random Forest-Based Classifier for Ramanome-Based Phage Susceptibility Test 
 ## Brief Description This repository contains the Python source code for calculating the **Composite Infection Index   (CII)**, a key algorithm developed for the research paper *"Rapid and Quantitative Phage Susceptibility Test by Ramanome"*. The CII is a Random Forest-based integrated index that quantitatively indicates whether a bacterial population is in a phage-infected or uninfected state based on Ramanomes. 
 ## Detailed Description ### Overview The CII algorithm is the core computational component of the proposed Ramanome-based phage susceptibility test. It processes preprocessed Ramanomes through a trained Random Forest model to generate a quantitative score. This score enables robust and binary classification of the bacterial physiological state. 
 ### Algorithm Function - **Input:** Preprocessed Raman spectra (e.g., smooth, baseline-corrected, normalized). - **Core Model:** A trained Random Forest classifier. - **Output:** The CII, a quantitative value where:   - **CII > high-cutoff ** strongly indicates a phage-infected state.   - **CII < low-cutoff ** strongly indicates an uninfected state. - **Application:** The CII value allows for quantitative and rapid determination of phage susceptibility, forming the basis of the novel diagnostic method presented in the associated paper. 
 ### Technical Implementation - **Programming Language:** Python 3.9 - **Dependencies:** Key libraries include `scikit-learn`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `joblib`/`pickle`. - **License:** [MIT License] 
 ### Relationship to Other Datasets This code is designed to be used in conjunction with the **Raw Raman Spectral Dataset** ( https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17470426 ) from the same study. The raw data should be preprocessed (as described in the paper's Methods section) before being input into this CII algorithm. 
 ### Related Publication This code supports the findings of the research article: **"Rapid and Quantitative Phage Susceptibility Test by Ramanome"** . Please cite both this software and the article when using this code.

# Source Code for the Composite Infection Index: A Random Forest-Based Classifier for Ramanome-Based Phage Susceptibility Test 
 ## Brief Description This repository contains the Python source code for calculating the **Composite Infection Index   (CII)**, a key algorithm developed for the research paper *"Rapid and Quantitative Phage Susceptibility Test by Ramanome"*. The CII is a Random Forest-based integrated index that quantitatively indicates whether a bacterial population is in a phage-infected or uninfected state based on Ramanomes. 
 ## Detailed Description ### Overview The CII algorithm is the core computational component of the proposed Ramanome-based phage susceptibility test. It processes preprocessed Ramanomes through a trained Random Forest model to generate a quantitative score. This score enables robust and binary classification of the bacterial physiological state. 
 ### Algorithm Function - **Input:** Preprocessed Raman spectra (e.g., smooth, baseline-corrected, normalized). - **Core Model:** A trained Random Forest classifier. - **Output:** The CII, a quantitative value where:   - **CII > high-cutoff ** strongly indicates a phage-infected state.   - **CII < low-cutoff ** strongly indicates an uninfected state. - **Application:** The CII value allows for quantitative and rapid determination of phage susceptibility, forming the basis of the novel diagnostic method presented in the associated paper. 
 ### Technical Implementation - **Programming Language:** Python 3.9 - **Dependencies:** Key libraries include `scikit-learn`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `joblib`/`pickle`. - **License:** [MIT License] 
 ### Relationship to Other Datasets This code is designed to be used in conjunction with the **Raw Raman Spectral Dataset** ( https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17470426 ) from the same study. The raw data should be preprocessed (as described in the paper's Methods section) before being input into this CII algorithm. 
 ### Related Publication This code supports the findings of the research article: **"Rapid and Quantitative Phage Susceptibility Test by Ramanome"** . Please cite both this software and the article when using this code.
